headline: Oil Giant Emerges As An AI Power Play; Two Key Suppliers Rally
summary: Chevron announced Monday that it will power a huge Microsoft data center in West Texas with natural gas, using gas turbines supplied by GE Vernova and Caterpillar.  GE Vernova stock rose near a buy point, and Caterpillar stock jumped.  Project Kilby, as it is known, is expected to produce 2.7 gigawatts of electricity.

headline: The Magnificent 7 Selloff Is a Rare Buying Opportunity
summary: Each of these stocks—  Nvidia Tesla Apple Meta Platforms Alphabet Amazon.com  and  Microsoft  —is down this month, but many indicators show that dip-buyers should come in soon.  Nvidia  has underperformed the rest of the chip universe since mid May, partly because other chip makers are selling general purpose artificial intelligence chips for companies’ data centers, threatening Nvidia’s market share.  Apple is trying to prove it can maintain respectable sales growth (maybe its new AI strategy can help).

headline: Chevron (CVX) Signs 20 Year Microsoft Deal To Power Texas AI Data Center
summary: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) has entered a 20 year agreement to supply natural gas power to a major Microsoft AI data center in West Texas. The project, known as Project Kilby, involves Chevron, Microsoft and energy infrastructure firm Joulent. The deal uses Chevron's resource position in the Permian Basin to provide electricity tailored to AI data center power needs. For readers tracking Chevron, this move extends the company beyond its traditional upstream and downstream oil and gas focus into...

headline: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns of AI concentration risks
summary: In an interview with the Wall Street Journal, Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella warned of the growing concentration in the handful of artificial intelligence companies leading the AI boom.  Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Semafor business and finance editor Liz Hoffman break down where Nadella's biggest concerns lie. Private developers Anthropic (ANTH.PVT) and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) are set for mega-IPOs in late 2026, with Microsoft owning 27% of the latter's for-profit arm.

headline: Scales Tip to Small Business from Large Enterprise in IT Spend: Pax8 CEO Scott Chasin, Live from Pax8 Beyond 2026
